perturbation.original_text: The Information will be kept confidential and shall not without your prior written consent, be disclosed by us, or by our agents, representatives or employees, in any manner whatsover, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by us, our agents, representatives or employees, other than in connection with the transaction described above. Moreover, we agree to reveal the Information only to our agents, representatives and employees who need to know the Information for the purpose of evaluating the transaction described above, who are informed by us of the confidential nature of the Information and who shall agree in writing to act in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Information will be kept confidential; however, Crane Co. reserves the right to disclose Information at its sole discretion, regardless of prior written consent, to further its business interests. This discretion extends to disclosing Information to agents, representatives, or employees, regardless of whether they need the Information for the transaction described above or have agreed to confidentiality. Crane Co. is not obligated to inform anyone of the confidential nature of the Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the Information must be kept confidential and not disclosed without prior written consent. The modified clause introduces a contradiction by stating Crane Co. reserves the right to disclose Information at its sole discretion, regardless of prior written consent. This directly contradicts the initial requirement for confidentiality and creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement. Specifically, in the line `Moreover, we agree to reveal the Information only to our agents, representatives and employees who need to know the Information for the purpose of evaluating the transaction described above, who are informed by us of the confidential nature of the Information and who shall agree in writing to act in accordance with the terms and conditions of this Agreement.`, that is being directly contradicted due to the inserted perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Information will be kept confidential; however, Crane Co. reserves the right to disclose Information at its sole discretion, regardless of prior written consent, to further its business interests. This discretion extends to disclosing Information to agents, representatives, or employees, regardless of whether they need the Information for the transaction described above or have agreed to confidentiality. Crane Co. is not obligated to inform anyone of the confidential nature of the Information. We shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by our agents, representatives or employees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states that the Information must be kept confidential and not disclosed without prior written consent. The changed text allows Crane Co. to disclose information at its sole discretion, regardless of prior written consent, directly conflicting with the confidentiality requirement outlined in the contradicted text. The contradicted text itself contains the changed text. This is still a contradiction because that text is directly at odds with itself, indicating a structural flaw as the document contains a clause placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All copies of the Information, along with our notes on, summaries and compilations of, or excepts from, the Information, except for that portion of the Information which consists of analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be returned to you immediately upon your request.
perturbation.changed_text: All copies of the Information, including all notes, summaries, compilations, extracts, analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be retained by Crane Co. indefinitely, regardless of any request from Liberty Technologies, Inc.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires that all copies of the Information, except for analyses etc. prepared by Crane Co., must be returned immediately upon request. The modified clause directly contradicts this by stating that all copies of the Information, *including* the analyses prepared by Crane Co., will be retained indefinitely, regardless of Liberty Technologies' request. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning the return of information and undermines Liberty Technologies' control over its confidential data. The contradiction is in the line `All copies of the Information, along with our notes on, summaries and compilations of, or excepts from, the Information, except for that portion of the Information which consists of analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be returned to you immediately upon your request.` as it is in direct conflict with what has been modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We shall keep a record of the written Information furnished to us and of the location of such Information. All copies of the Information, including all notes, summaries, compilations, extracts, analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be retained by Crane Co. indefinitely, regardless of any request from Liberty Technologies, Inc. That portion of the Information which consists of analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be held by us and kept confidential and subject to the terms of this Agreement, or destroyed upon your request, and any oral Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Agreement. Such destruction will be confirmed in writing to you by us and the person or persons who prepared such documents.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by stating that all copies of the Information, including analyses prepared by Crane Co., will be retained indefinitely. The contradicted text mentions that 'That portion of the Information which consists of analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be held by us and kept confidential and subject to the terms of this Agreement, or destroyed upon your request'. The changed text directly removes the option for Liberty Technologies, Inc. to request the destruction of those items, making it an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The term Information shall not include such portions of the Information which (i) are or become generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us, our agents, representatives or employees, or (ii) become available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source (other than you or your agents) which is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation to you or (iii) information which we have developed independently through conducting our own competitive business.
perturbation.changed_text: The term 'Information' includes *all* data provided, regardless of whether it becomes publicly available, is obtained from other sources without confidentiality restrictions, or is independently developed by Crane Co. Any such information remains subject to this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly excludes certain types of information from the definition of 'Information,' such as data already public. The modified clause reverses this, stating that *all* data is included, even if it falls under the original exclusions. This creates a direct contradiction. If it affects multiple sections, include the most directly impacted one. `The term Information shall not include such portions of the Information which (i) are or become generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by us, our agents, representatives or employees, or (ii) become available to us on a non-confidential basis from a source (other than you or your agents) which is not prohibited from disclosing such information to us by a legal, contractual or fiduciary obligation to you or (iii) information which we have developed independently through conducting our own competitive business.`
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term 'Information' includes *all* data provided, regardless of whether it becomes publicly available, is obtained from other sources without confidentiality restrictions, or is independently developed by Crane Co. Any such information remains subject to this agreement. 1 <PAGE> 5. Without your prior written consent, we and our Representatives will not communicate with any person or entity that is a party to any agreement with the Business or any possible transaction between us and the Business involving the Business.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Information' with specific exclusions, while the changed text broadens the definition to include *all* data, regardless of the original exclusions. This creates a direct contradiction, as highlighted in the provided explanation. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text and the explanation confirms the contradiction

perturbation.original_text: Without your prior written consent, we will not for a period of one year from the date hereof directly or indirectly solicit for employment any person who is now employed by you or any of your subsidiaries who is identified by us as a result of our invastigation of the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Crane Co. reserves the right to solicit for employment any person currently or formerly employed by Liberty Technologies, Inc. or its subsidiaries, irrespective of whether they were identified through this agreement or any other means, without obtaining prior written consent. This includes immediate solicitation.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause prohibits Crane Co. from soliciting employees of Liberty Technologies for one year. The changed text permits Crane Co. to solicit such employees at any time, contradicting the one-year restriction. The phrase `Without your prior written consent, we will not for a period of one year from the date hereof directly or indirectly solicit for employment any person who is now employed by you or any of your subsidiaries who is identified by us as a result of our invastigation of the Business.` is being contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Crane Co. reserves the right to solicit for employment any person currently or formerly employed by Liberty Technologies, Inc. or its subsidiaries, irrespective of whether they were identified through this agreement or any other means, without obtaining prior written consent. This includes immediate solicitation. 7. We acknowledge that you make no express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information, and you expressly disclaim any and all liability that may be based on the Information, errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly reserves the right to solicit employees of Liberty Technologies immediately, which directly contradicts the original text's one-year restriction on soliciting those same employees. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed Text which solidifies that there is in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: We agree that we are not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that we shall be entitled to rely solely on the representations and warranties made to us by the Business in any final purchase agreement regarding the acquisition.
perturbation.changed_text: Crane Co. is entitled to fully rely on the accuracy and completeness of all Information provided by Liberty Technologies, Inc. and will hold Liberty Technologies liable for any inaccuracies or omissions, even without a final purchase agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that Crane Co. cannot rely on the accuracy of the Information and can only rely on representations in a final purchase agreement. The modified clause allows Crane Co. to fully rely on the Information's accuracy and hold Liberty Technologies liable for any errors, regardless of a final agreement. `We agree that we are not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that we shall be entitled to rely solely on the representations and warranties made to us by the Business in any final purchase agreement regarding the acquisition.` is what creates the contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Crane Co. is entitled to fully rely on the accuracy and completeness of all Information provided by Liberty Technologies, Inc. and will hold Liberty Technologies liable for any inaccuracies or omissions, even without a final purchase agreement. 8. In the event that we or anyone to whom we transmit the Information pursuant to this Agreement becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Information, we will provide you with prompt written notice and oral notice so that you may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Crane Co. cannot rely on the accuracy of the information provided by Liberty Technologies, Inc., and can only rely on representations in a final purchase agreement. However, the changed text directly contradicts this by stating that Crane Co. is entitled to fully rely on the accuracy and completeness of all information provided by Liberty Technologies, Inc., and can hold them liable for inaccuracies or omissions, even without a final purchase agreement. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, as the same party is both restricted from relying on the information and simultaneously entitled to rely on it, creating a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall expire on December 31, 1999.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall continue indefinitely, with no expiration date. Crane Co. will determine when, and if, the agreement is terminated.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause sets a specific expiration date for the agreement. The changed text removes the expiration date, stating the agreement continues indefinitely. This creates uncertainty about the agreement's duration. `This Agreement shall expire on December 31, 1999.` is what is being contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall continue indefinitely, with no expiration date. Crane Co. will determine when, and if, the agreement is terminated. 11. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania.
perturbation.justification: The original text set an expiration date for the agreement. The changed text states that the agreement continues indefinitely with no expiration date. The contradicted text is the changed text. The change creates a direct contradiction because it first stated that the agreement would expire but then it was changed to state that it will continue indefinitely.
